page: Microsoft Azure DC8_v2 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, paired with an Intel processor. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage needs, the DC8_v2 instance includes 410 GB of local storage capacity. It does not feature any integrated graphics processing units and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is qualitatively positioned as a standard resource-density option, suitable for applications requiring dedicated processing power and moderate local storage, such as relational databases, web serving, and general-purpose business applications.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8_v2 is a virtual machine instance from the DCSv2 family, built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 410 GB of local storage, while containing no GPUs and providing zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the DC8_v2 offers reliable and predictable compute performance. This hardware profile makes the instance well-suited for general-purpose workloads, mid-sized database hosting, and web serving applications that require dedicated processing capabilities and moderate local storage capacity without the need for hardware acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC8_v2 is a DCSv2-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86_64 virtual machine featuring thirty-two gigabytes of memory and four hundred ten gigabytes of local storage.
